title: hibbertia guttata
intro: Hibbertia guttata, also known as the yellow buttercup or native yellow guinea flower, is a flowering plant native to Australia, belonging to the family Cunoniaceae. It has been traditionally used in Australian herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Hibbertia guttata are thought to include soothing irritated skin and mucous membranes, as well as providing antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of Hibbertia guttata may include teas, tinctures, and infusions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
